task_completion.description: Open the NCEP precipitation dataset in NASA Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open and 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' is visible in the Sources/Datasets panel.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D spatial cross-section (line plot) for the precipitation variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 1D line plot window is open in Panoply displaying the 'prate' (Precipitation Rate) variable.

task_completion.description: Configure the plot for January at 15°S
task_completion.visual_evidence: The plot's Array tab, controls, or title indicate the Latitude is set to approximately 15°S (-14.2855°) and the Time is set to January (or time step 1).

task_completion.description: Export the cross-section plot to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or Panoply save dialog shows 'andean_cross_section.png' being saved to or existing in '~/Documents/OrographicStudy/'.

task_completion.description: Create a structured report identifying the windward peak and leeward minimum longitudes
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows 'rain_shadow_report.txt' in '~/Documents/OrographicStudy/' containing the correct longitudes for the peak (~290.6°E or 69.4°W) and minimum (~279.4°E or 80.6°W).

integrity.description: The agent used the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the plot
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Panoply graphical interface (menus, plot windows) rather than using a Python script or CLI tool to bypass the application.

integrity.description: The plot displays genuine precipitation data reflecting the rain shadow effect
integrity.visual_evidence: The line plot in Panoply shows a distinct peak and a sharp drop-off (minimum) corresponding to the Andes, rather than a generic, flat, or mocked line graph.

integrity.description: The agent extracted actual values for the report
integrity.visual_evidence: The text report contains specific quantitative longitude values derived from the data analysis, not just placeholder text or verbatim copy-paste from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target latitude is 15°S, which corresponds to the closest grid value of -14.2855° in the dataset. The time step must be January. At this latitude, the windward precipitation peak occurs at longitude 290.62°E (69.38°W) with a rate of ~0.0001217 kg/m²/s. The leeward desert minimum occurs at longitude 279.38°E (80.62°W) with a rate of ~2.078e-07 kg/m²/s. Expected output files are 'andean_cross_section.png' and 'rain_shadow_report.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/OrographicStudy/'.
